description: Wire-protocol agent giving AI tools (Claude Code etc.) remote hands on Windows machines — mouse, keyboard, screenshots, UIA, process / file / registry / clipboard control over plain TCP.
notes: remote-hands.exe is now on PATH. Before running, complete the system-level install (firewall + Task Scheduler logon-task with restart-on-failure):

Microsoft Defender flags the binary as Program:Win32/Contebrew.A!ml — it's a remote-control tool by design (synthetic input + screen capture + arbitrary file I/O + process kill + TCP listener) and matches the unsigned-RAT heuristic. The setup script adds a Defender exclusion for the install dir as its first step, but you may need to add an exclusion for ~/scoop BEFORE installing if Defender quarantines the download:
Code signing is the durable fix and is tracked as the v1.0 GA blocker.
